primary.notes: Single-draft cohort limits LI-6 to a neutral default. Strongest dimensions are LI-1/LI-2/LI-3/LI-5 (all 8); LI-4 is held at 7 by the 'Lesson:' close, which is borderline LinkedIn-AI cadence. No HARD FLOOR triggers, no platform-specific cap activations. Numbers (149/25/9) verify against the locked v2 lens catalog. Hashtag count=3 in ideal band; char_count=785 in SHORT_TAKE bracket.

per_criterion.rationale: Voice is first-person, story-led, and grounded in lived work ('Last quarter I spent two weeks watching...', 'By the third day I realized'). Tone is thoughtful-authority rather than contrarian — no 'Most marketers don't realize...' opener, no aggressive declarative. The realization is framed as something JR noticed about his own positioning, which lands as B2B-buyer-friendly insight. 'Lens catalog' is mild jargon but gets immediate plain-English follow-up via the 149/25/9 enumeration. Slight slip toward formulaic register at the 'Lesson:' close — it doesn't break voice but signals lesson-extraction cadence rather than JR's natural reflective close. Otherwise consistent.

SOURCE claims trace: '149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames' aligns with the locked v2 catalog (memory confirms 149+25+9 figures); attribution 'gofreddy lens catalog v2 locked 2026-04-23' matches. INTERPRETIVE claim ('my own brand narrative was incoherent') is framed as JR's observation, not asserted as fact. Lived-work claim names a real entity (gofreddy) — assumed present in voice.md given it's JR's own company. The 'we ship to clients' phrasing is general ('clients' plural) rather than vague-specific ('we shipped X for [unnamed]'), so the LinkedIn cap-at-7 trigger doesn't fire. No HARD FLOOR violations. Could be marginally stronger with one more named anchor (a client or a specific About-page version), which keeps it from a 9.

First sentence is ~95 chars, well inside the 210-char show-more reference. It's story-led with concrete grounding (timeframe 'last quarter', specific action 'watching the audit run', mild surprise 'finish before lunch' implying speed/competence). Earns line two by setting up 'By the third day I realized it was scoring my own positioning' — the realization beat. No contrarian declarative, no '🤔 Are you ready' bait, no engagement-prompt close. Identifies JR as someone with lived experience without declaring it. Not a 9 because the hook leans on company-watching rather than a sharper before-the-fold tension that pulls reader past line 2 with stakes.

No 'Game-changer.', no 'Here's what I learned.' close, no 'Thoughts? 👇', no engagement-bait emoji, no 4+ consecutive newlines for whitespace padding. Close is 'Lesson: when your audit is good enough to fairly score your own marketing, it is good enough to ship.' — this is borderline. The 'Lesson:' framing has lesson-extraction cadence adjacent to LinkedIn-AI 'thought-leadership' patterns; it doesn't trip a banned regex but the format is recognizable. One soft pattern slipping through; otherwise voice is consistent and rhythm reads as JR-typed.

char_count=785 sits inside SHORT_TAKE bracket (500-900). Structure fits the bracket: story-opening hook → substantive observation paragraph (About-page incoherence) → bridge paragraph anchoring authority via lens catalog → closing thought. Hashtags: 3 targeted tags (B2BMarketing, MarketingAudit, AIagents) — in the [3,5] ideal range, all map to JR's brand pillars (no generic '#marketing' alone). No pad-to-length filler; each paragraph carries weight. Not 9 because the structure is well-executed but not architecturally distinctive — competent SHORT_TAKE rather than a structurally inventive one.

Cohort contains a single draft (drafts/short_take.md). With N=1, narrative-archetype variance and pillar spread cannot be meaningfully evaluated — no streak to detect, no diversity to reward. Per the rubric this is a cross-item dimension scored as the geometric mean of per-draft cohort-fit scores; a singleton cohort defaults to neutral. The single draft is story-led/lesson-led hybrid on the B2B-marketing pillar, which is consistent with its declared metadata. Score reflects data-unavailable / single-draft cohort rather than judgment about quality.

secondary.notes: Strong short-take draft for LinkedIn voice and opening. Main risks are unverifiable grounding because voice.md is not supplied, vague client/project specificity, and missing cohort evidence for LI-6.

per_criterion.rationale: Mostly reads like LinkedIn thoughtful authority: first-person, story-led, and grounded in lived work. Evidence: "Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch" and "By the third day I realized..." The tone is not hot-take-y. Minor deduction for dense jargon in "149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames" without enough plain-English explanation.

The draft names gofreddy and gives attribution in metadata, but the required voice.md reference is not present in the supplied artifacts, so the lived-work grounding cannot be fully verified. It also makes a specific first-person claim, "the same lens catalog we ship to clients," without naming any client or project, which triggers the LinkedIn vague-specificity cap pressure.

The opener earns the next line. It is story-led, first-person, and concrete: "Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch." It establishes lived-work authority before the cutoff without sounding like contrarian bait.

No obvious LinkedIn-AI tells appear. There is no engagement-bait close, no emoji prompt, no fake hot-take framing, and whitespace is functional. The only slight formulaic note is the explicit "Lesson:" close, but it still fits the post's cadence better than a generic AI-style takeaway.

For SHORT_TAKE, the structure works: story opening, substantive middle, clear closing thought, and 3 targeted hashtags in metadata: "B2BMarketing, MarketingAudit, AIagents." The hashtags fit the brand/topic. Minor deduction because the middle leans on internal terminology that may narrow accessibility.

Only one draft is supplied, so cohort-level archetype and pillar variation cannot be demonstrated. The visible draft is story-led and uses one voice pillar, "B2B-marketing." Since no other drafts are in front of the judge, the cohort shows no spread across archetypes or pillars.
